ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Has there reached you the report of the Overwhelming [event]?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has the news of the Overwhelming Event reached you ˹O Prophet˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Some] faces, that Day, will be humbled,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day ˹some˺ faces will be downcast,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Working [hard] and exhausted.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹totally˺ overburdened, exhausted,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will [enter to] burn in an intensely hot Fire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: burning in a scorching Fire,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will be given drink from a boiling spring.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: left to drink from a scalding spring.

ayahs.textArabic: لَّيۡسَ لَهُمۡ طَعَامٌ إِلَّا مِن ضَرِيعٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laysa lahum taAAamun illa min dareeAAin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For them there will be no food except from a poisonous, thorny plant
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will have no food except a foul, thorny shrub,

ayahs.textArabic: لَّا يُسۡمِنُ وَلَا يُغۡنِي مِن جُوعٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yusminu wala yughnee min jooAAin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Which neither nourishes nor avails against hunger.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: neither nourishing nor satisfying hunger.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Other] faces, that Day, will show pleasure.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day ˹other˺ faces will be glowing with bliss,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: With their effort [they are] satisfied
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹fully˺ pleased with their striving,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In an elevated garden,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: in an elevated Garden,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Wherein they will hear no unsuitable speech.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: where no idle talk will be heard.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Within it is a flowing spring.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In it will be a running spring,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Within it are couches raised high
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: along with thrones raised high,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And cups put in place
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and cups set at hand,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And cushions lined up
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹fine˺ cushions lined up,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And carpets spread around.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹splendid˺ carpets spread out.

ayahs.textArabic: أَفَلَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَى ٱلۡإِبِلِ كَيۡفَ خُلِقَتۡ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Afala yanthuroona ila alibili kayfa khuliqat
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then do they not look at the camels - how they are created?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do they not ever reflect on camels—how they were ˹masterfully˺ created;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And at the sky - how it is raised?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the sky—how it was raised ˹high˺;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And at the mountains - how they are erected?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the mountains—how they were firmly set up;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And at the earth - how it is spread out?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the earth—how it was levelled out?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So remind, [O Muhammad]; you are only a reminder.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So, ˹continue to˺ remind ˹all, O Prophet˺, for your duty is only to remind.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You are not over them a controller.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You are not ˹there˺ to compel them ˹to believe˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: However, he who turns away and disbelieves -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But whoever turns away, persisting in disbelief,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then Allah will punish him with the greatest punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then Allah will inflict upon them the major punishment.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, to Us is their return.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely to Us is their return,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then indeed, upon Us is their account.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then surely with Us is their reckoning.
